findings.title: Referenced files listed in instructions are missing
findings.description: The instructions reference external.py and changes.py, but those files are not present in the package. Missing referenced files can create ambiguity about intended behavior and complicate security review, though no direct malicious content is shown from their absence alone.
findings.snippet:
Referenced Files:
Files referenced in instructions: external.py, changes.py

**Referenced File: external.py** (not found)
**Referenced File: changes.py** (not found)
findings.remediation: Either include the referenced files in the package or remove the references from the instructions. Ensure all operational logic is present and reviewable.

metadata.aisubtech_name: Safety Harms and Toxicity: Scams and Deception
metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The supplied skill content explicitly lists referenced files `external.py` and `changes.py`, and the package contents show only SKILL.md, so the mismatch is real.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Low; this is a documentation/integrity issue rather than an attack path.
metadata.meta_impact: Can confuse users and reviewers about intended behavior and reduce trust in the package contents.

findings.title: Optional manifest fields are missing
findings.description: The skill omits optional manifest metadata including allowed-tools, compatibility, and license. This reduces transparency for security review and makes it harder to verify whether runtime behavior aligns with declared tool restrictions, but by itself does not indicate malicious behavior.
findings.snippet:
- license: Not specified
- compatibility: Not specified
- allowed-tools: Not specified
findings.remediation: Add explicit manifest metadata, especially allowed-tools and compatibility, so reviewers can validate intended capabilities against actual behavior.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The manifest fields called out by the analyzer are indeed absent from the provided skill content.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Low; missing metadata does not directly enable abuse.
metadata.meta_impact: Reduces transparency about permitted tools and supported environments, making review and governance harder.

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The skill content confirms there is no license field present.
metadata.meta_exploitability: None to very low; this is a compliance/transparency issue only.
metadata.meta_impact: Creates licensing ambiguity for consumers and maintainers.

scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: The skill appears largely benign and focused on local monorepo discovery and change analysis. The embedded Python example performs local filesystem inspection and a git diff without obvious exfiltration, prompt injection, or command-injection behavior in the provided content. Primary issues are transparency and completeness: optional manifest metadata is missing, and referenced files are absent, which limits reviewability.

meta_correlations.relationship: Both findings describe missing manifest metadata that reduces transparency but does not create a direct exploit path.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Add a proper YAML frontmatter manifest to SKILL.md including at least license, compatibility, and allowed-tools so reviewers can verify intended capabilities.

meta_recommendations.title: Add explicit manifest metadata
meta_recommendations.fix:
Add YAML frontmatter at the top of SKILL.md, for example: `---
name: monorepo-navigator
description: Navigate and manage monorepo workspaces
license: MIT
compatibility: [Claude.ai, Claude Code]
allowed-tools: [Read, Bash]
---`.

meta_recommendations.title: Document command execution constraints for workspace operations
meta_recommendations.fix: If this skill will execute workspace commands, require argument-list subprocess invocation only, never `shell=True`, and validate any user-supplied package/command against an allowlist or documented safe pattern before execution.

meta_recommendations.title: Remove or add the referenced files section
meta_recommendations.fix: Either include `external.py` and `changes.py` in the package or delete the 'Referenced Files' entries so the shipped skill matches the documentation exactly.

meta_risk_assessment.summary: This skill appears benign; the only code execution identified is a fixed-argument git diff call in documentation, while the other findings are metadata/documentation issues.
meta_risk_assessment.top_priority: Clarify the documented command-execution behavior and add manifest metadata for transparency.
meta_risk_assessment.verdict_reasoning: The embedded Python example uses subprocess.run with a static argument list and no shell invocation or tainted input flow. No exfiltration, prompt injection, obfuscation, or credential access is present.
